current_state.root_cause_missing: Files that never had --source-headers written. The populate pass only writes to files with existing headers when --overwrite is set; files with no header at all are always eligible for first-time write.
current_state.root_cause_no_layer: Bootstrap gap: @layer/@capability come from reading existing file headers. Files with no prior annotation have empty layer in ElementData, so generateHeadersFromElements emits no @layer. Path-inference in the generator is the only way to break the cycle without LLM enrichment.

risks.title: Path-inference produces wrong layer for edge-case files
risks.mitigation: Inference rules use a priority-ordered match list; unmatched files get no @layer rather than a wrong one. Validated by running --dry-run check before writing.

risks.title: First-time header pass writes to files that should be excluded
risks.mitigation: populate-coderef respects .coderefignore and the scanner's existing file exclusion rules. No new exclusion logic needed.

decision_records.decision: Path-inference rules derive @layer from file path patterns only
decision_records.rationale: LLM enrichment (Lloyd) is a separate concern and not available in CI. Path patterns are deterministic, auditable, and can be expressed as a simple ordered match table.
Only 1,883 files have missing headers. Files with existing headers (513 defined, 30 partial) should not be touched in Phase 1 to avoid disturbing LLM-annotated tags already present.
